events.name: Tthe Archdeacon of Richmond summoned Sir Ivo de Etton for impounding 400 sheep at Gilling; Sir Ivo thought he had the right to do this. The jurors found that a certain field was enclosed for the people of the township from Michaelmas to Martinmas, and that the Archdeacon’s shepherd had placed his sheep in the field before Martinmas and this was wrong. They therefore decided that Sir Ivo was justified in impounding the sheep.
He and the other residents of Gilling were levied subsidies for the conduct of the Scottish Wars."

Thomas, son of John of Wymbleton, was put in the stocks by Sir Ivo, and brought an action for assault. The defendant pleaded that Thomas, being a villein, had refused to perform his duty of working at the harvest, and therefore he had put him in the stocks “as he had a perfect right to do.” The plaintiff, Thomas, maintained that he was a “free man of free status and condition and therefore not liable to the service in question."
Adam Louther sued Ivo in the Crown Court in a dispute between the Church and the Lord of the Manor over ownership of Gilling land.
granted Gilling manor to his son Thomas

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill是William de Etton, Lord of Gilling的兒子，於1242年出生在英國越可爾。他於1298年與Mrs. Joan De Etton在英國英格蘭越可爾 諾斯 利丁結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1235年被列為constable of tikhill。他去世於大約1315年在英國英格蘭越可爾也達爾享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Etton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Elton (Berkshire, Cheshire, Derbyshire, Durham, Herefordshire, Huntingdonshire, Lancashire, and Nottinghamshire). The Eltons in Chesire, Derbyshire, and Durham are from Old English ǣl ‘eel’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. Elton in Berkshire is from the Old English female personal name Æthelflǣd (composed of the elements æthel ‘noble’ + flǣd ‘beauty’). Both Elton in Herefordshire and in Nottinghamshire are from the Old English personal name Ella + tūn. The Berkshire origin for the surname is especially well represented.
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in eastern Norway, in Old Norse Elgjartún ‘enclosed sacred place’ (or fenced in for elg ‘moose’?).

nameMeanings.name: Ivo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Form of   Yves   used in Germany and occasionally in the English-speaking world. It represents the nominative case of the Latinized form of the name.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill was born in 1242, in Yorkshire, England as the son of William de Etton, Lord of Gilling. He married Mrs. Joan De Etton in 1298, in Yorkshire North Riding, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. In 1235, his occupation is listed as constable of tikhill. He died about 1315, in Ryedale, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo d’Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill est né en 1242, à Everwyck, Angleterre, fils de William d’Etton, Lord of Gilling. Il a épousé Mrs. Joan De Etton en 1298, à Yorkshire North Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. En 1235, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of tikhill. Il est décédé environ 1315, à Ryedale, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans.

Er starb um 1315 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Ryedale, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhillは1242年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでWilliam de Etton, Lord of Gillingの息子として生まれた。1298年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア ノース リディングでMrs. Joan De Ettonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1235年，職業はconstable of tikhillと記録されている。彼は約1315年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアリエダルで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill(은)는 1242년 영국 요르크실르에서 William de Etton, Lord of Gilling의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 노르스 리딩에서 Mrs. Joan De Etton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1235년, 그의 직업은 constable of tikhill이었다. 그는 대략 1315년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리들일에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Mrs. Joan De Etton em 1298, em Yorkshire North Riding, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Sir Ivo de Etton, Lord of Gilling, Constable of Tikhill родился в 1242 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. Его отца звали William de Etton, Lord of Gilling. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Joan De Etton в 1298 г. в местности еркшир норт ридинг, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. В 1235 году он работал по профессии constable of tikhill. Он умер около 1315 г. в местности риедеил, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет.
